clusters.title: Bus carrying 40 students crashes into tree in Sydney's west, driver critical
clusters.neutral_headline: Bus carrying 40 students crashes into tree in Sydney's west, driver critical

items.title: Bus carrying 40 students crashes into tree in Sydney's west, driver critical
items.standfirst: The bus driver, a man believed to be in his 50s, is suspected to have suffered a medical episode before the crash.
items.content: A school bus carrying more than 40 teenage students has crashed in Sydney's west, leaving the driver in a critical condition and several students injured.Emergency crews were called to Erskine Park Road at Erskine Park about 1pm today after the bus veered off the road and crashed into a tree.Police said the bus was carrying a teacher and 41 students in years 11 and 12.READ MORE: Con-woman Melissa Caddick's victims share 'small pot' last cashParamedics treated a number of students at the scene, with five teenagers aged between 16 and 18 taken to hospital for treatment.One is believed to have suffered fractures.A NSW Ambulance spokesperson said the driver, a man believed to be in his 50s, is suspected of having suffered a medical episode before the crash.READ MORE: Australia is one of the richest countries in the world. But household debt is making us feel 'poor'He became trapped inside the vehicle for a short time before emergency crews freed him from the wreckage.The driver was taken to Westmead Hospital in a critical condition.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.content: A school bus carrying more than 40 teenage students has crashed in Sydney's west, leaving the driver in a critical condition and several students injured.

clusters.title: Photographer who allegedly stole, pawned Bondi terror victim's camera identified
clusters.neutral_headline: Photographer who allegedly stole, pawned Bondi terror victim's camera identified

items.title: Photographer who allegedly stole, pawned Bondi terror victim's camera identified
items.standfirst: The victim's wife spent five months searching for her husband's camera so she could look at the images the 61-year-old took in the moments before his tragic death.
items.content: The identity of the photographer who allegedly stole and pawned the camera gear of a slain Bondi terror victim in the aftermath of the attack has been revealed.Police allege that Danny James Ridley stole the camera equipment of 61-year-old retired police officer and photographer Peter Meagher during the terror attack, when 15 innocent people – including Meagher – were killed.Following a raid at Ridley's family home in Sydney's west yesterday, police will allege the 35-year-old, who was also a photographer at the Hannukkah event, stole the equipment and pawned it for $800 just three days later.READ MORE: Australia ranks among the richest countries. So why do we feel 'poor'?In March, Meagher's widow, Virginia, issued a plea on social media for any information about the missing camera after police and his family were unable to find it, and it was deemed stolen shortly after. Virginia spent five months searching for her husband's camera so she could look at the images the 61-year-old took in the moments before his tragic death. It is now alleged that the SD card inside Meagher's camera was found at Ridley's Marayong home. READ MORE: $52 billion bank giving 'lower-value human capital' the flick In the raid, heavily armed police seized a camera, handcuffs, and electronic devices, while a car search yielded a "small amount of white crystal powder" and other electronics.Ridley was arrested and charged with multiple theft, drug, and weapon offences.He was bailed and will return to court on June 22.A total of 15 innocent people were killed on the evening of Sunday, December 14, when two men opened fire on the crowd of people attending the outdoor event.One of the attackers, who police say was 50-year-old Sajid Akram, was killed by police, while his son, Naveed Akram, was injured and arrested, and remains before the courts.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here

items.title: 'Shocking': Wife of Bondi victim reacts as man accused of stealing camera
items.standfirst: A photographer who allegedly stole a camera from a Bondi terror attack victim before pawning it days later has been charged.
items.content: A photographer who allegedly stole a camera from a Bondi terror attack victim before pawning it days later has been charged.

clusters.title: Woman dies after being hit by police car
clusters.neutral_headline: Woman dies after being hit by police car

items.title: Woman dies after being hit by police car
items.standfirst: An investigation has begun after the incident at 7am this morning.
items.content: A woman has died after she was hit by a police car in Cairns this morning.According to a police statement, a police vehicle collided with the 71-year-old woman from Trinity Beach on the corner of Sheridan and Lily Street at around 7am.The officers provided immediate medical assistance to the woman, and she was taken to Cairns Hospital in a critical condition.READ MORE: Penny Wong condemns Israeli minister after taunting video emergesShe was later declared dead in hospital. Police are investigating at the scene, with the Forensic Crash Unit and the Ethical Standards Command on site, with oversight from the Crime Corruption Commissioner and Coroner.NEVER MISS A STORY: Get your breaking news and exclusive stories first by following us across all platforms.Download the 9NEWS App here via Apple and Google PlayMake 9News your preferred source on Google by ticking this box hereSign up to our breaking newsletter here
